description: Focus on emotional triggers, stress responses, and impulse control

questions.scenario: You feel a sudden tightness in your chest when checking your phone and seeing a message from a specific person.

options.text: Open the message immediately to get it over with
Notice the physical sensation and name the emotion before opening
Ignore the message until you feel less anxious
Delete the message without reading it

questions.scenario: You are watching a movie and suddenly feel tears forming during an unexpected scene. You are with friends.

options.text: Suppress the tears and make a joke to deflect
Allow the emotion and wonder what specifically triggered it
Leave the room until you compose yourself
Pretend you have something in your eye

questions.scenario: You notice you have been checking social media every 10 minutes for the past hour without conscious decision.

options.text: Continue scrolling since you are already distracted
Pause and identify what emotion you are avoiding or seeking
Set a timer to limit future social media use
Delete the apps immediately to remove temptation

questions.scenario: You wake up at 3 AM with your mind racing about an unresolved conflict from yesterday.

options.text: Try to force yourself back to sleep by suppressing the thoughts
Identify the specific emotion beneath the racing thoughts
Get up and start planning your response to the conflict
Write down the thoughts to process them tomorrow

questions.scenario: You feel an inexplicable urge to eat something sweet after a stressful phone call, even though you are not hungry.

options.text: Eat the sweet thing since you deserve it after stress
Notice the urge and ask what emotion the sweetness would soothe
Find a healthier snack to satisfy the urge
Drink water to suppress the craving

questions.scenario: You feel your mood shift dramatically when entering a specific room in your house, but you cannot explain why.

options.text: Avoid that room when possible
Explore what memory or association might be triggered by the space
Redecorate the room to change its energy
Assume it is just random and ignore it

questions.scenario: Someone cuts in front of you in a long line after you have been waiting 20 minutes. You feel rage surge.

options.text: Confront them immediately about cutting the line
Notice the rage and choose whether saying something serves you
Say nothing but stew in resentment for the rest of the wait
Make a loud comment to the person behind you about line-cutters

questions.scenario: You receive a text that triggers you immediately - your hands shake and you want to fire back a harsh response.

options.text: Type and send your immediate reaction to get it off your chest
Put the phone down and wait until your hands stop shaking
Type a draft but do not send it yet
Ignore the text and pretend you did not see it

questions.scenario: You are about to make an expensive impulse purchase online. Your finger hovers over the buy button.

options.text: Buy it now before you talk yourself out of it
Pause and ask what emotion you are trying to buy away
Add it to cart and sleep on it overnight
Buy it but set a rule to return it if unused in 30 days

questions.scenario: You are in an argument and the other person says something that hits exactly your insecurity. You want to hurt them back.

options.text: Say the hurtful thing you know will wound them equally
Notice your hurt and choose not to escalate, even if you need to step away
Shut down and stop responding to end the argument
Tell them they hit below the belt and you are done talking

questions.scenario: You have been sober for three months and suddenly feel an intense craving at a social event where others are drinking.

options.text: Have just one drink to take the edge off
Leave the event immediately and call your support person
Stay but obsessively focus on resisting the craving
Tell everyone about your struggle to get their support

questions.scenario: You are on a diet and someone offers you your favorite food that you have been avoiding for weeks.

options.text: Take it since you have been good and deserve a treat
Check in with your body to see if you are actually hungry
Take a small portion to be polite
Refuse and explain your diet in detail

questions.scenario: You see a stranger crying on a park bench. You feel an urge to help but also feel awkward about intruding.

options.text: Walk past and respect their privacy
Sit nearby and quietly ask if they need someone to talk to
Offer them a tissue without saying anything
Tell them it will be okay and keep walking

questions.scenario: Your friend is telling a story and you realize they are embellishing details to make themselves look better.

options.text: Call them out on the inaccuracies in front of others
Wonder what insecurity might be driving their need to embellish
Correct the story with the real version
Let it go since it does not harm anyone

questions.scenario: A child is having a tantrum in a store and their parent looks overwhelmed and embarrassed.

options.text: Judge the parent for not controlling their child
Offer a kind word or helping hand to the parent
Ignore the situation completely
Give the parent a knowing look of solidarity

questions.scenario: Your partner is anxious about an upcoming event and keeps asking for reassurance. You are starting to feel frustrated.

options.text: Tell them to stop worrying and just deal with it
Recognize their anxiety is real to them even if it frustrates you
Give the same reassurance again even though you are tired of repeating it
Suggest they talk to someone else about it

questions.scenario: You are watching a news story about a tragedy in a country you have never visited. You feel numb and switch channels.

options.text: Keep switching - you cannot help everyone
Notice your numbness and allow yourself to feel the tragedy briefly
Donate to a related cause to take action
Tell yourself it is too far away to be real

questions.scenario: A friend shares a problem you solved easily last year. You feel impatient with their struggle.

options.text: Tell them exactly how to fix it based on your experience
Remember that their journey is different from yours and listen
Change the subject to avoid your impatience
Share your story to show them it gets better

questions.scenario: You are at a family dinner and two relatives start arguing about politics. The tension is escalating.

options.text: Join the argument with your own strong opinion
Redirect by asking about a shared interest or changing the subject
Stay silent and hope it blows over
Suggest agreeing to disagree and moving on

questions.scenario: You need to tell a friend that their new partner makes you uncomfortable, but you do not want to damage the friendship.

options.text: Be direct and tell them you do not like their partner
Share your specific observations without making it about your dislike
Say nothing and avoid spending time with them as a couple
Ask if they have noticed any concerning behaviors

questions.scenario: You are at a party where you do not know anyone. You have been standing alone for 10 minutes.

options.text: Stay on your phone to look busy until someone approaches
Find someone else alone and introduce yourself
Leave early since you are not enjoying yourself
Join a group conversation and listen until you find an entry point

questions.scenario: Your neighbor plays loud music late at night regularly. You need your sleep but want to maintain good relations.

options.text: Bang on the wall or call the police to file a complaint
Visit them at a calm time and explain your situation collaboratively
Buy earplugs and suffer in silence
Leave a passive-aggressive note on their door

questions.scenario: A friend asks you to be a reference for a job you do not think they are qualified for.

options.text: Give a lukewarm reference that hints at your reservations
Have an honest conversation about your concerns before agreeing
Lie and give a glowing reference to preserve the friendship
Decline by saying you are not the right person to reference them

questions.scenario: You are in a group project and one member is not contributing. The deadline is approaching.

options.text: Do their work yourself to ensure quality
Check in with them privately to understand what is blocking their contribution
Call them out in the group chat to pressure them
Report them to the project lead without talking to them first
